html:
April 13, 2026

DC Industry News Digest

Daily intelligence on data center market moves, expansion deals, regulatory shifts, and community sentiment — curated for developers, investors, and operators.

$700B
Hyperscaler Capex
2.7 GW
Largest New Project
$64B
Projects Blocked
65%
Public Opposition
🔴

Critical & Breaking

3 stories
Market

Hyperscaler Capex Projected at $700B — Nearly 6x 2022 Levels

Moody's Ratings via Data Centre Magazine

The six largest US hyperscalers are projected to spend ~$700B this year. Unprecedented capex surge signals continued aggressive build-out despite supply chain headwinds and community pushback.

Market

Half of 2026 Data Center Pipeline May Not Materialize

Sightline Climate

Up to 50% of announced pipeline faces delays or cancellation due to power constraints, permitting challenges, and equipment shortages — a critical signal for deal flow and site availability.

Market

US Build-Out Hinges on Hard-to-Find Electrical Equipment

Bloomberg

AI data center expansion increasingly relies on Chinese electrical equipment imports. Supply chain bottlenecks for transformers and switchgear are forcing developers to get creative with sourcing.

🏗️

Expansion & Deals

7 stories
Expansion

Tallgrass & Crusoe's Project Jade: 2.7 GW AI Capacity

Data Center Knowledge

One of the largest single AI infrastructure projects announced. This mega-campus could reshape regional power markets and signals continued appetite for massive-scale AI training facilities.

Expansion

Aligned Data Centers Raises $2.58B for US Expansion

Data Center Knowledge

Financing targets Dallas, Phoenix, and Northern Virginia — the three hottest US markets. Capital remains highly available for well-positioned operators with execution track records.

Expansion

Related Digital's $1.2B, 302 MW Facility

Data Center Knowledge

Real estate giant Related Companies continues its push into digital infrastructure, reflecting accelerating cross-sector capital flows into the data center space.

Expansion

Meta's $800M Project Cosmo + Entergy Louisiana Deal

Data Center Knowledge

715K sq ft hyperscale facility with a utility partnership projected to deliver ~$2.65B in customer savings over 20 years — a model for utility-hyperscaler cooperation.

Expansion

Microsoft Playing Catch-Up in Data Center Build-Out

Bloomberg

Microsoft accelerating construction cadence to keep pace with AI compute demand. Intensifying competition among hyperscalers for available sites and power capacity.

Expansion

Manta Subsea Cable Selects MDC for Mexico Landing Hubs

DataX Connect

New subsea connectivity linking Mexico City, Querétaro, Bogotá, Panama City, and the US. Low-latency routes could accelerate Latin American data center demand.

Expansion

Vietnam's First AI Data Center (200 MW) Breaks Ground

CSG Talent

Vietnam enters the AI infrastructure race, reflecting global diversification of investment beyond traditional North American and European markets.

🏛️

Regulatory & Policy

4 stories
Regulatory

The DATA Act of 2026

Foley Hoag LLP

New federal legislation reshaping data center permitting, environmental review, and grid interconnection frameworks. Could significantly impact development timelines and compliance.

Regulatory

FERC Developing Rules for Large-Load Grid Connections

Engineering News-Record

FERC extending its regulatory role over how data centers connect to transmission grids. Hyperscalers want speed; utilities worry about grid overload. Outcome directly affects site selection.

Regulatory

Maryland Advancing New Data Center Regulations

Maryland Matters

Following extensive community pushback, Maryland moves forward with new requirements. A bellwether for mid-Atlantic markets where density is already high.

Regulatory

EPA Enforcement: xAI Memphis Operated Without Air Permits

Data Center Knowledge

xAI operated methane gas turbines without permits for 18+ months. Signals tightening environmental oversight — operators should audit compliance proactively.

🏘️

Community Sentiment

3 stories
Community

65% of Americans Oppose AI Data Centers in Their Communities

The AI Insider / Quinnipiac University

Broad, bipartisan opposition. $64B in projects blocked or delayed. Opposition is 55% Republican, 45% Democrat — this is not a partisan issue and must be factored into every site plan.

Community

Pennsylvania: 68% Would Oppose Local Data Center Development

City & State Pennsylvania

Strong opposition across party lines in PA (53% R, 81% D, 67% I). Developers targeting PA sites should budget for extended community engagement timelines.

Community

Nationwide AI Data Center Boom Stirs Resistance

CBS News

CBS spotlight amplifies growing national resistance. The industry remains largely unregulated at the federal level with 4,000+ facilities operating. Mainstream coverage is raising visibility.

⚙️

Technology & Partnerships

2 stories
Technology

BCG: Solving the US Data Center Power Crunch

Boston Consulting Group

New analysis outlines strategies for power constraints: renewable integration, micro-grids, long-life batteries, and demand-side management. A roadmap for operators facing interconnection delays.

Technology

Data Center World 2026: 500+ Exhibitors, Startup Challenge

Data Center World / BusinessWire

DCW 2026 (April 20–23, Washington DC) features 20%+ exhibitor growth with 24 startups pitching live. Tracks cover Emerging IT, Sustainability, Cloud Innovation, and Power Sourcing.

🎯 Key Takeaways for Deals & Pipeline

1
Power is the bottleneck — and the opportunity. With FERC expanding its role, equipment strained, and half the pipeline at risk, sites with secured power and near-term interconnection command massive premiums.
2
Community opposition is a material deal risk. $64B blocked, 65% national opposition. Every site selection process must now include community sentiment analysis and extended engagement timelines.
3
Regulatory landscape is tightening fast. The DATA Act, FERC rules, Maryland regulations, and EPA enforcement all point to more compliance requirements. Factor longer permitting timelines into projections.
4
Capital is abundant but increasingly selective. $700B hyperscaler capex and deals like Aligned's $2.58B raise show money is flowing — but toward proven markets and operators with track records.
5
Global diversification accelerating. Vietnam, Paraguay, and Mexico subsea hubs signal that LatAm and SEA markets are maturing. International pipeline opportunities are expanding.

DC Industry News Digest • Published April 13, 2026

Curated from 40+ industry sources • Powered by DC Hub

Note: DC Hub API was unavailable during this run. Content sourced via web search fallback.
linkedin_text:
The data center industry is at an inflection point. Here's what our team is watching this week:

📊 $700B in hyperscaler capex projected for 2026 — nearly 6x what was spent in 2022. The build-out is accelerating, but so are the headwinds.

⚡ Half the 2026 pipeline may not materialize. Power constraints, equipment shortages (especially transformers and switchgear), and lengthening permitting timelines are real bottlenecks. Sites with secured interconnection are commanding massive premiums.

🏗️ Major moves this week:
→ Tallgrass & Crusoe's Project Jade: 2.7 GW of AI capacity
→ Aligned Data Centers raises $2.58B for Dallas, Phoenix & NoVA expansion
→ Meta's $800M Project Cosmo + $2.65B utility savings deal with Entergy Louisiana
→ Vietnam breaks ground on its first 200 MW AI data center
→ Manta subsea cable selects MDC for Mexico landing hubs

🏛️ Regulatory landscape is shifting fast. The DATA Act of 2026, new FERC rules for large-load grid connections, and EPA enforcement actions (xAI Memphis) all signal tighter oversight ahead.

🏘️ Community opposition is now a material deal risk — 65% of Americans oppose data centers in their communities, and $64B in projects have been blocked or delayed. This is bipartisan and it's not going away.

We publish a daily DC Industry News Digest tracking these developments. If you're in data center development, investment, or operations — this is the intel that matters.

#DataCenters #AI #Infrastructure #RealEstate #Energy #DataCenterDevelopment
